blocks.text: Jan 30 added one new resolution: Pat Cummins’s replacement was Ben Dwarshuis, which we had included but underweighted. I’m scanning Jan 30 articles now for the Jan 31 and early-February questions, especially Australian Open, Grammys, Ukraine/Balochistan, and market/crash incidents.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, re
path='articles/2026/01/30/articles.jsonl'
print('exists', os.path.exists(path))
if not os.path.exists(path): raise SystemExit
lines=0; usable=0; bydate={}
for line in open(path):
    lines+=1
    a=json.loads(line); dp=a.get('date_publish') or ''
    if dp <= '2026-01-30': usable += 1
    bydate[dp]=bydate.get(dp,0)+1
print('lines',lines,'usable<=Jan30',usable,'latest_dates', sorted(bydate)[-10:])
checks={
'391811 miners bus': lambda t: ('miner' in t and 'bus' in t and ('dnipropetrovsk' in t or 'drone' in t or 'ukrain' in t)),
'562701 Balochistan': lambda t: 'balochistan' in t and ('secured' in t or 'three-day' in t or 'police' in t or 'battle' in t or 'militant' in t),
'946810 Grammys AOTY': lambda t: ('album of the year' in t and ('grammy' in t or 'grammys' in t)) or ('grammys' in t and ('lady gaga' in t or 'kendrick lamar' in t or 'bad bunny' in t)),
'951896 Australian men': lambda t: ('australian open' in t and ('sinner' in t or 'alcaraz' in t or 'djokovic' in t or 'zverev' in t)),
'492629 Harden Clippers': lambda t: 'james harden' in t or ('harden' in t and 'clippers' in t and 'trade' in t),
'955662 Brazil bus crash': lambda t: ('bus crash' in t and ('brazil' in t or 'minas gerais' in t or 'bahia' in t or 'parana' in t) and ('16' in t or 'people' in t)),
'976004 Libya forensic': lambda t: ('forensic' in t and 'libya' in t) or 'libyan killing probe' in t,
'995305 market fire': lambda t: ('market' in t and 'fire' in t and ('huge' in t or 'massive' in t or 'large' in t or 'major' in t or 'engulf' in t)),
'102687 Norway PM corruption': lambda t: ('norwegian' in t and 'prime minister' in t and ('corruption' in t or 'gross' in t or 'police' in t)) or 'erna solberg' in t,
'449104 minerals': lambda t: ('minerals stockpile' in t) or ('critical minerals' in t and ('reserve' in t or 'stockpile' in t)),
'336425 Ukraine market shelling': lambda t: ('market shelling' in t) or ('market' in t and 'shelling' in t and ('ukraine' in t or 'ukrainian' in t or 'kostiantynivka' in t or 'kupiansk' in t)),
'220862 League Cup': lambda t: ('league cup final' in t or 'carabao cup final' in t) and ('manchester city' in t or 'man city' in t or 'city' in t),
'243685 Myanmar panel': lambda t: ('five-member panel' in t and 'myanmar' in t) or ('national security and peace commission' in t) or ('myanmar' in t and 'panel' in t),
'426743 Saif burial': lambda t: 'saif al-islam' in t and ('buried' in t or 'burial' in t or 'funeral' in t or 'killed' in t),
'453736 Libya killed': lambda t: ('mohammed al-haddad' in t or 'saif al-islam gaddafi' in t or 'khalifa haftar' in t) and ('killed' in t or 'death' in t or 'libya' in t),
'579252 Saleh city': lambda t: 'saleh mohammadi' in t or ('wrestler' in t and 'public execution' in t),
'198725 Narges exile': lambda t: 'narges mohammadi' in t and ('exile' in t or 'sentenced' in t or 'zanjan' in t or 'qazvin' in t),
'456284 WaPo publisher': lambda t: ('washington post' in t and 'will lewis' in t) or ('publisher' in t and 'washington post' in t),
'534224 downhill women': lambda t: ('women' in t and 'downhill' in t and ('olympic' in t or 'milan-cortina' in t)),
'180958 Super Bowl': lambda t: ('super bowl' in t and ('seahawks' in t or 'patriots' in t or 'seattle' in t or 'new england' in t)),
}
arts=[]
for line in open(path):
    a=json.loads(line)
    if (a.get('date_publish') or '')>'2026-01-30': continue
    title=a.get('title') or ''; c=a.get('content') or ''
    t=(title+'\\n'+c).lower()
    arts.append((a,title,c,t))
for label,fn in checks.items():
    hits=[]
    for a,title,c,t in arts:
        if fn(t):
            idx=0
            # choose a useful distinctive token
            for kw in ['miner','balochistan','album of the year','australian open','james harden','bus crash','forensic','market','norwegian','critical minerals','shelling','league cup','myanmar','saif','mohammadi','narges','washington post','downhill','super bowl']:
                j=t.find(kw)
                if j"'!=-1:
                    idx=j; break
            snippet=(title+'"' | '+c[max(0,idx-350):idx+1050]).replace('\\n',' ')
            hits.append((a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),snippet[:1500]))
            if len(hits)>=5: break
    print('\\n##',label,'hits',len(hits))
    for dp,src,sn in hits:
        print('-',dp,src,sn)
PY"

On the sidelines of the event, India and Canada signed a joint statement renewing the India-Canada Ministerial Energy Dialogue, signalling intent to deepen cooperation across LNG, LPG and crude oil, alongside cleaner technologies, critical minerals, storage, electricity systems and the application of artificial intelligence in the energy sector. The agreement positioned India as a long-term demand anchor and Canada as a reliable supplier, with reciprocal investment and business-to-business partnerships playing key enablers. Natural gas stood out as the most immediate test case for this collaborative logic. In the leadership panel Repositioning natural gas and energy transformation: pragmatic bridging resource to pivotal destination fuel, industry leaders examined the role of gas and LNG in strengthening energy resilience while supporting near-term emissions reduction through coal-to-gas switching. The discussion reflected broad consensus that natural gas is increasingly being seen not only as a transition fuel, but as a long-term component of modern energy systems. For India, panelists pointed to the rapid expansion of pipelines, LNG terminals, regasification capacity and city gas distribution networks, while also flagging affordability as the principal constraint on wider adoption. - 2026-01-30 www.salon.com Turning Point's Super Bowl alternative to Bad Bunny looks like a flop | ecause Bad Bunny doesn’t like Immigration and Customs Enforcement, an opinion shared by 63% of American voters. Advertisement: TPUSA mistook the racist spleen-venting on X for a real trend and announced they would put on their own halftime show. “They forced the free hand of the market to create an alternative,” TPUSA spokesman Andrew Kolvet announced in October. There was about a week of hype around this totally awesome, blow-your-socks-off MAGA music extravaganza, but since then there’s been, well, nothing. For months, the website for the “All-American Halftime Show” did not change. There was only a survey asking people to vote for what genre of music they’d like to see, with options we’re apparently meant to believe will excite the youth, like “Anything in English” and “worship music.” But this week, TPUSA finally updated the site — not with a dramatic unveiling of a lineup, but with just a list of the media outlets streaming the program, such as One America News and the Daily Wire. Instead of a list of performers, there are a series of traps to lure you into donating or signing up for TPUSA’s email list. One doesn’t need to be a marketing genius to suspect that this does not bode well for the quality of the upcoming show. Typically, when promoters seek to draw viewers into a televised music program, they will heavily advertise the big name stars they are featuring, believin

Performers include Justin Bieber, Lady Gaga, Sabrina Carpenter, and Tyler, the Creator, with a special segment dedicated to Best New Artist nominees. - 2026-01-30 www.fastcompany.com Why Sabrina Carpenter is eating Pringles at the Super Bowl | he big game is an exciting partnership that hits all the right factors for us.” Pringles’ celebrity strategy Pringles is no stranger to the celebrity Super Bowl ad game. The brand tapped Meghan Trainor in 2023, Chris Pratt in 2024, and last year, a seemingly random collection of stars like James Harden, Adam Brody, and Nick Offerman. But Reinecke says the brand’s strategy isn’t some game of big name roulette. “Each partnership has sharpened our approach,” she says. “We’ve learned that the most effective talent isn’t just recognizable—it has to authentically align with both the audience and the brand’s voice.” Reinecke points to Carpenter as an example of this. “She connects deeply with Gen Z while naturally embodying the self-aware, unhinged internet humor that defines how the brand shows up today,” Reinecke says. “The partnership has really embodied a shared sense of play, which we hope ultimately makes it resonate with our fans.” Beyond the Game Given the level of investment required to just get a spot in the game, most brands now extend Super Bowl-related work to run long before and after the final whistle. For Pringles, that means tying its work with Carpenter into an existing campaign that launched last fall to resurrect its ‘90s ad slogan “Once You Pop.”

- 2026-01-30 www.thenation.com How Milk Became a Battleground in Trump’s War on “Woke” |  helped absorb the surplus, embedding milk into the daily lives of American children. In the 1970s, facing yet another glut, the federal government began purchasing excess milk and converting it into cheese; within a decade, it was sitting on hundreds of million pounds of it. Unable to find a market solution, the government distributed the surplus—sometimes moldy—to food pantries and low-income families. But as time went on, Americans still kept drinking less and less milk per day. The United States Department of Agriculture estimates that between 1970 and 2019, milk consumption per day dropped nearly 50 percent. Milk drinking overall decreased more than 10 percent in just the decade between 2014 and 2024, with the steepest drop in consumption coming from children, according to the agency. - 2026-01-30 www.koreaherald.com Seoul unveils blueprint to become global leader in 'physical AI' |  is slated to begin in 2028, while the Suseo cluster is to be completed by 2030. To strengthen the industrial ecosystem, Seoul will launch a test bed demonstration center in the second half of this year and invest 100 billion won ($70 million) by 2030 to support real-world trials and market entry. The plan includes embedding physical AI into logistics, mobility and urban operations, while constructing infrastructure capable of collecting real-time city data. The city will also invest 70 billion won in R&D and operate a 150 billion won Seoul Vision 2030 Fund to nurture promising companies. Seoul plans to deploy physical AI technologies across transportation, care services and safety. Level 4 unstaffed robotaxis are set to begin operating in October, while the city's early-morning autonomous shuttle bus service will expand from one route to four. Rehabilitation robots, wearable assistive exoskeletons, AI fire-patrol robots and safety-inspection drones will be introduced across welfare and disaster-response fields. Seoul said it will invest 120 billion won in smart infrastructure management by 2030. Reaffirming the city's commitment to responsible AI

TITLE Pakistan military raids kill 41 armed fighters in Balochistan DATE 2026-01-30 SRC www.aljazeera.com

KW first raid :  of 4Gunmen in Pakistan kidnap, kill nine bus passengers, officials say list 2 of 4Israel is trying to hijack the Baloch struggle list 3 of 4One year floods, the next drought: Farmers battle Pakistan’s climate plight list 4 of 4How Pakistan misread the Taliban and lost peace on the frontier end of list Despite the incident’s proximity to the border with Afghanistan, the military statement claimed – without providing evidence – that the deadly encounters involved fighters backed by India. In the first raid, at least 30 fighters were killed on the outskirts of Balochistan’s Harnai district, Pakistan’s Dawn newspaper quoted the military as saying. The fighters belonged to Fitna-al-Khawarij (a term the government uses to refer to the banned Pakistan Taliban (TTP), which is allied with Afghanistan’s Taliban), the army officials said. In a separate operation in Balochistan’s Panjgur district, “11 India-sponsored terrorists” belonging to the Fitna-al-Hindustan were killed, the military’s media wing Inter-Services Public Relations said. The military added that ammunition as well as money allegedly looted during a bank robbery in Panjgur in December were recovered from the fighters killed in the second raid. Both raids took place on Thursday. None of the soldiers involved was killed. India has yet to comment on the allegations. The Pakistani military added that “sanitisation operations” were ongoing in both districts to eliminate any remaining fighters. Pakistani President Asif Ali Zardari said the operation “reflects the state’s unwavering resolve,” according to Pakistan’s Express Tribune. The newspaper also quoted Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif as saying that “the entire nation stands with the armed forces in this ongoing fight against terrorism”. Pakistan has seen a surge in violence in recent months, largely blamed on Baloch separatist groups such as the banned Balochistan Liberation Army and the TTP. In January, Pakistan’s security forces killed 12 armed fighters accused of planning to carry out a hostage attack in Balochistan’s Kharan district. The military also claimed that the fighters were backed by India in that incident.

TITLE What to expect at the 2026 Grammy Awards DATE 2026-01-30 SRC www.independent.co.uk
Stay up to date with notifications from The Independent Notifications can be managed in browser preferences. Jump to content Thank you for registering Please refresh the page or navigate to another page on the site to be automatically logged inPlease refresh your browser to be logged in Swipe for next article Independent Bulletin homepage Download our Social Partner We are 8 logo (opens in a new tab) What to expect at the 2026 Grammy Awards Roisin O'Connor Friday 30 January 2026 16:19 GMT The Independent offers predictions for the 'Big Four' categories at the upcoming 2026 Grammy Awards, with Olivia Dean, Chappell Roan and Bad Bunny all in strong contention. British artist Olivia Dean is tipped to win Best New Artist, praised for her successful second album and timeless yet relevant music. Chappell Roan's 'The Subway' is predicted to take home Record of the Year, recognised for its superb production and poignant storytelling. Despite Chappell Roan's 'The Subway' being a strong contender, Rosé and Bruno Mars' commercially successful 'APT' is expected to win Song of the Year. Bad Bunny's Debí Tirar Más Fotos is forecast to win Album of the Year, celebrated as an intelligent, complex, and genre-blending masterpiece.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json, glob
phrases=['secured town','town secured','three-day battle','three day battle','Balochistan town','Pakistani police say', 'police said they had secured']
for p in phrases:
    hits=[]
    for path in sorted(glob.glob('articles/2026/01/*/articles.jsonl')):
        for line in open(path):
            a=json.loads(line)
            if (a.get('date_publish') or '')>'2026-01-30': continue
            text=((a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')).lower()
            if p.lower() in text and ('baloch' in text or 'pakistan' in text):
                hits.append((path,a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),a.get('title') or ''))
                if len(hits)>=5: break
        if len(hits)>=5: break
    print('\\n',p, '=>')
    for h in hits: print(h)
PY"

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import csv,json,os
from collections import Counter
print('state_exists', os.path.exists('state.json'))
if os.path.exists('state.json'):
    print(json.dumps(json.load(open('state.json')), indent=2)[:5000])
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv', newline='')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='false']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='true']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active_without_prediction',sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction'] or r['my_prediction']=='None'))
print('active_pred_dates',Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] for r in active).most_common(20))
print('\\nResolved on/after 2026-01-30:')
for r in resolved:
    if r['resolution_date']>='2026-01-30':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'],'GT',r['ground_truth'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
print('\\nActive upcoming <=2026-02-08:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-08':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'])

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import csv,json,os
from collections import Counter
print('state_exists', os.path.exists('state.json'))
if os.path.exists('state.json'):
    print(json.dumps(json.load(open('state.json')), indent=2)[:5000])
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv', newline='')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='false']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='true']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active_without_prediction',sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction'] or r['my_prediction']=='None'))
print('active_pred_dates',Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] for r in active).most_common(20))
print('\\nResolved on/after 2026-01-30:')
for r in resolved:
    if r['resolution_date']>='2026-01-30':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'],'GT',r['ground_truth'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
print('\\nActive upcoming <=2026-02-08:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-08':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
PY"
